chapter_context.summary: John writes to Gaius, commending his hospitality to traveling teachers. He warns against the prideful Diotrephes and commends the good testimony of Demetrius.

outline.text: Commendation of Gaius for Walking in Truth
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Commendation of Gaius for Walking in Truth (1:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 3 John.

outline.text: Warning Against Diotrephes and Commendation of Demetrius
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Warning Against Diotrephes and Commendation of Demetrius (1:9-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 3 John.
This section focuses on Final Greetings (1:13-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 3 John.

1.commentary:
### Tyndale Study Notes
**Verse Insight: 3 John 1:1**
John writes to Gaius, a beloved brother who is commended for his faithfulness.
1.full_commentary:
### Matthew Henry Commentary
Matthew Henry offers devotional reflections on the spiritual impact of this passage for the believer.

### Jamieson-Fausset-Brown
JFB analysis explores the historical context and original language nuances of this portion of the New Testament.

key_themes.title: Hospitality
key_themes.description: Supporting those who work for the truth.

devotional.question: How does this passage impact your daily walk with God?

cultural_historical.title: New Testament Context
cultural_historical.text: Written in the 1st century AD to diverse groups of believers across the Roman Empire.
